memoryArtifacts.headline: Family of Herbert from Annals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777
memoryArtifacts.description: Family of Herbert from Annuals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777. However, it is noted, that Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) was not the Count of Vermandois, but rather his uncle was, Herbert IV, Count of Vermandois. It is also noted that the son of Herbertus Camerarius, Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer) is missing from the Annals and Antiquities, but his grandson Herbert (Herbert FitzHenry) and great grandson Herbert  (Herbert FitzHerbert) are included.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer)
memoryArtifacts.description: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer). The record skips to the son of Henry, Herbert FitzHenry (Herbert FitzHerbert I, and his son Herbert FitzHerbert (II).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbert於1364年出生在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾帕可萊迪斯時，他的父親Gwilym ap Jenkin37歲，母親Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan29歲。他於1379年與Ann Wallis在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1438年7月8日在他的家鄉享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbert于1364年出生在英国威尔斯蒙姆爾帕可萊迪斯时，他的父亲Gwilym ap Jenkin37岁，母亲Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan29岁。他于1379年与Ann Wallis在英国威尔斯蒙姆爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1438年7月8日在他的家乡享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, and French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In Britain, this Old French name, introduced by the Normans, reinforced the less common Old English cognate Herebeorht. The surname was taken to Ireland after the Anglo-Norman invasion and in the 16th century.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the German personal name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Harbison
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name Herbert (the change from -er- to -ar- was a common one in Old French and Middle English). An intrusive n later gave Harbinson .

variants.name: Hebard
meanings: English (Surrey):
variant of Herbert , with assimilation of the first r. Compare Hebb .
variant of   Hibbert  .

variants.name: Harbert
meanings: German and English: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbers
meanings: German and Dutch: patronymic from   Herbert  . It is found mainly in Lower Saxony and across the Dutch border.

variants.name: Dickherber
meanings: Probably a nickname for a fat person by the name of Herbert , of which Herber is a short form, or, more likely, in the north from Die(c)kherber, a topographic and distinguishing name for a Herber living by the dike, see Dicke 2. Compare Dickmeyer .

variants.name: Hebert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Pierre, Lucien, Michel, Gaston, Alcide, Camille.
French (Hébert): assimilated form of Herbert . Compare Abair , Abar , Abare , Abear , Aber , Eber , and Ebert .
German: variant of   Heber   1.

variants.name: Herber
meanings: German and Dutch: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbertson
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name   Herbert  .

variants.name: Hebb
meanings: English (East Yorkshire and northeast Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Hebb, Ebb, pet forms of Herbert .

variants.name: Herb
meanings: South German: nickname for a harsh or rough person, from Middle High German herb ‘tart, harsh’.
German: from the personal name Herpo, a pet form of Herbert .

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Hywell
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Gwilyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Gwilim
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Gwillym
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbert was born in 1364, in Parcletis, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Gwilym ap Jenkin, was 37 and his mother, Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan, was 29. He married Ann Wallis in 1379, in Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 8 July 1438, in his hometown, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbert est né en 1364, à Parcletis, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gwilym ap Jenkin, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Ann Wallis en 1379, à Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 8 juillet 1438, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbert 1364 in Parcletis, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gwilym ap Jenkin 37 und seine Mutter Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan 29. Er hat 1379 in Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom Ann Wallis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 8. Juli 1438 im Alter von 74 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbert è nato il nel 1364 nel Parcletis, Monmouthshire, Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Gwilym ap Jenkin, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Ann Wallis nel 1379, nel Monmouthshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 8 luglio 1438, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbertが1364年にイギリスウェールズモンモウスシュアイアパークレティスで生まれた時，父Gwilym ap Jenkinは37歳，母Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychanは29歳であった。1379年にイギリスウェールズモンモウスシュアイアでAnn Wallisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1438年7月8日に故郷で74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbert(이)가 1364년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르 파르클티스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gwilym ap Jenkin(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1379년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르에서 Ann Wallis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1438년 7월 8일 그의 고향에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbert родился в 1364 г. в местности парклетис, монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gwilym ap Jenkin было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann Wallis в 1379 г. в местности монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 8 июля 1438 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hywel ap Gwilyn Herbert nació en 1364, en Parcletis, Monmouthshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Gwilym ap Jenkin, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Ann Wallis en 1379, en Monmouthshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 8 de julio de 1438, en su pueblo, a la edad de 74 años.
